title: prunella vulgaris subvar. reptans
intro: Prunella vulgaris subvar. reptans, also known as self-heal or heal-all, is a perennial plant in the mint family that grows in temperate regions worldwide, characterized by its small purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of self-heal include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help combat infections. Self-heal can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in salves and creams for topical application to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
